catalyst.catalyst_summary: AI hyperscale data centers require 20,000-40,000 tonnes of copper each (transformers, busbars, cooling, cables). At $350B+ combined hyperscaler AI capex in 2026, the implied incremental copper demand by 2028 equals ~7% of current global mine supply. This demand is NOT in mainstream sell-side copper models — the market still prices copper as an EV/China construction metal. The mispricing window closes when Goldman/JPMorgan update their base case models to include DC demand.
catalyst.novelty_assessment: The EV copper narrative is fully priced. The China construction floor is priced. The AI data center angle is in specialist reports only (WoodMac, CRU, Morgan Stanley commodities Q3 2025) but absent from generalist buy-side copper coverage. The market has not made the connection between $350B AI capex and physical copper. This is the thesis. Secondary driver: struct_energy_transition_grid provides an independent demand floor via HVDC cable buildout that reinforces the supply tightness argument independently of AI.

sector.rationale: Miners (COPX) over physical copper (COPA.L) for three reasons: (1) operational leverage — a 10% copper price move produces 20-30% move in miners via margin expansion, amplifying the re-rating when it comes; (2) the thesis is an equity story, not a commodity spot story — we are betting on narrative closing in analyst models, which re-rates the sector multiple, not just the spot price; (3) current AISC ($2.75/lb FCX) vs spot ($4.60/lb) = $1.85/lb margin is near a decade high — exceptional margin makes miners cheap relative to the commodity on P/CF basis. Not gold_miners or lithium_miners: copper is the only metal with simultaneous structural demand from AI infra, grid buildout, and EV without a viable supply response in the <5yr horizon.

vehicle.currency_risk_note: 4COP es la versión XETRA del Global X Copper Miners UCITS ETF (mismo ISIN IE0003Z9E2Y3 que COPX.L). Fondo denominado en USD, listado en XETRA. EUR/USD ~1.14. Copper cotiza en USD — un USD fuerte compensa parcialmente la depreciación EUR/USD. No se cubre: el catalizador (AI capex US) es USD-bullish, creando una cobertura natural parcial.
vehicle.vehicle_selection_rationale: CHANGED v3 2026-06-04: Vehículo actualizado de COPX.L (LSE) a 4COP (XETRA). Mismo fondo, mismo ISIN IE0003Z9E2Y3, misma exposición subyacente. Diferencia clave: 4COP es ACUMULATIVO (dividendos reinvertidos) vs distributing — fiscalmente mejor para inversor español al no generar rentas del capital anuales. AUM €1.1B (dato actualizado vs $380M previo — el fondo ha crecido). TER 0.55%. Disponible en Revolut. COPA.L sigue excluida: replica cobre spot 1:1 (swap sintético) y no captura la re-rating de equity que es el alpha del thesis.

entry.trigger_description: RECALIBRATED v2 2026-06-03: Copper has moved from ~$10,200 to ~$13,965/tonne since thesis drafted. Entry parameters updated to current market. Enter when BOTH conditions are met within a 10-trading-day window: (1) COPX.L price does not fall more than 8% from current level (confirms supply tightness sustained); (2) COPX.L vs COPA.L ratio rising for 2 consecutive weeks (miners outperforming commodity = equity re-rating signal, not just commodity beta). Original AUM trigger replaced: AUM flow is lagging (chases price); miner/commodity ratio is leading. If copper breaks above $15,000 before entry, reassess upside — the commodity price may already be pricing DC demand.
entry.entry_price_limit_note: UPDATED 2026-06-04 from live yfinance data. COPX.L current price: $70.12. Entry limit set at $73.50 (+5% above current = allows entry on breakout but not chasing a gap). COPX at $90.25 for reference (non-UCITS). The 1M acceleration (+14.2%) and supply shock catalyst (Grasberg force majeure) create a valid breakout entry. Rule: do not enter if COPX.L has gapped above $75 without a pullback — wait for consolidation.
entry.entry_price_actual_note: Ejecutado vía Revolut en 4COP (XETRA). Confirmar precio exacto de ejecución con broker. Referencia: COPX.L a $70.12 en snapshot 2026-06-04; equivalente 4COP ~€61.50 al cambio EUR/USD 1.14.
entry.position_size_rationale: ENTERED at 6% (€1,000) on 2026-06-04 (Tier 2 ceiling 8%). RECALIBRATED to 3.5% target by monthly review 2026-06-04 — a partial REDUCE, not a full exit. Two reasons: (1) The near-term supply-tightness leg broke — asm_02 is breaching (LME stock 393,400t vs the 200,000t monitoring line; inv_03's 350,000t review_and_reduce threshold crossed on the latest reading). The structural DC-copper-demand thesis is intact, but the spot-scarcity support is gone, so the position no longer warrants top-of-tier sizing. (2) Portfolio correlation: this position plus the open grid thesis (4%) jointly ride the AI→power→copper complex and summed to 10%, breaching the Tier-2 8% combined ceiling. Trimming copper 6%→3.5% brings the complex to 7.5% (≤8%). ACTION FOR USER: sell ~42% of the 4COP position (~€420 of the ~€1,000), leaving ~€580 ≈ 3.5%. Re-add toward 6% only if (a) LME inventories resume falling below 200kt AND (b) Q2 hyperscaler earnings (Jul-Aug 2026) confirm AI capex above guidance.

assumptions.statement: Combined hyperscaler AI capex (Microsoft + Google + Amazon + Meta) stays above $60B/quarter guidance for each of the next 3 quarterly earnings cycles.
assumptions.measurability: Source: quarterly earnings transcripts and 10-Q/10-K filings. Check each earnings release (Q2 Jul, Q3 Oct, Q4 Jan). Falsified by: any single quarter where combined guidance falls below $60B AND the revision vs prior quarter exceeds -15%. A single miss is noise; a guide-down trend is signal.

assumptions.statement: LME copper warehouse inventory stays below 200,000 tonnes on a 4-week rolling average throughout the thesis horizon.
assumptions.measurability: Source: LME daily warehouse inventory report (public, free). Compute 4-week rolling average monthly. Falsified by: 4-week rolling average exceeds 200,000 tonnes for 8 consecutive weeks. Rationale: inventory rising above 200k signals either supply normalization or demand destruction — both undermine the supply-tightness argument that supports the price floor.
assumptions.status_note: ⚠ BREACHING (monthly review 2026-06-04): LME copper warehouse stock = 393,400t as of 2026-05-20 with positive net inflows (+1,275t/day), per MacroMicro/westmetall. This is ~2x the 200,000t monitoring line and ABOVE the inv_03 review_and_reduce threshold (350,000t). The prior project value of 98,000t ('multi-year lows') was erroneous. Strict falsification of this assumption requires the 4-week rolling average to stay >200,000t for 8 consecutive weeks — currently week 1/8, so status remains 'monitoring' per its own rule, NOT yet 'invalidated'. But the near-term supply-tightness leg the thesis leaned on is gone: inventories are rising, not scarce. Combined with S&P analysts flagging copper as 'overextended', this triggers the partial reduction recorded in entry.position_size_rationale.

assumptions.statement: COPX basket average AISC stays below $3.50/lb — proxied by FCX + SCCO combined quarterly reports.
assumptions.measurability: Source: FCX and SCCO quarterly earnings. Check each quarterly release. Falsified by: both FCX AND SCCO report AISC > $3.50/lb in the same quarter. Rationale: FCX alone is no longer a clean proxy — Grasberg Block Cave incident (Sep 2025) will increase FCX-specific AISC in H2 2026 due to production volume cut (3.4B → 3.1B lbs 2026). SCCO (Southern Copper, ~20% of COPX) has lower costs and provides balance. Using both avoids a company-specific issue invalidating a sector-wide assumption.
assumptions.status_note: FCX Q1 2026: net cash cost $1.91/lb, estimated AISC ~$2.30-2.50/lb — well below threshold. BUT Grasberg production cut announced: H2 2026 output guidance cut 9%. Watch FCX Q2/Q3 2026 AISC trend carefully. If FCX AISC rises above $3.50/lb, check SCCO before triggering — may be company-specific rather than sector-wide.
assumptions.grasberg_risk_note: Grasberg Block Cave (FCX, Indonesia): Accident Sep 2025 (7 workers). Wet ore conditions persist. Production capacity capped at ~85% until late 2027. FCX cut 2026 guidance to 3.1B lbs from 3.4B. Morgan Stanley downgraded FCX. This is COMPANY-SPECIFIC risk — does not affect SCCO, Antofagasta, Lundin (other major COPX holdings). Monitor FCX weight in COPX (~15-20%).

assumptions.statement: China Caixin Manufacturing PMI does not fall below 49.0 for 2 consecutive months.
assumptions.measurability: Source: Caixin PMI release (monthly, around 1st of month). Falsified by: 2 consecutive months below 49.0. RECALIBRATED v2 2026-06-03: Original threshold was 47.0 / 3 months — too conservative. PMI at 52.2 (April) and 51.8 (May) 2026 shows PMI at 5-year high; the thesis would not be threatened until PMI reaches contraction zone (below 50), not deep contraction (below 47). New threshold: <49.0 for 2 months signals moderate manufacturing slowdown that copper demand would feel. This is now a MONITORING threshold (early warning), not a crisis threshold. Add secondary signal: if Caixin PMI drops below 49 for 1 month AND LME inventory rises above 150,000T simultaneously, escalate to at_risk.
assumptions.status_note: April 2026: 52.2 (5-year high). May 2026: 51.8. Both well above new 49.0 threshold. China manufacturing in expansion — macro override risk absent.

invalidation_conditions.condition: LME copper spot price closes below $11,000/tonne for 10 consecutive trading days.
invalidation_conditions.recalibration_note: RECALIBRATED v2 2026-06-03: Original $8,500 was ~17% below $10,200 (thesis draft price). At current $13,965, the equivalent ~21% drawdown would be ~$11,040. Rounded to $11,000. This represents a meaningful correction that would signal either: (a) AI capex cycle reversal, (b) China demand destruction, or (c) major supply surprise. At $11,000 copper, the structural thesis is still intact but the near-term trade timing is broken.

invalidation_conditions.condition: Any 2 of the 3 largest hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Amazon) cut AI capex guidance by >25% vs prior quarter in the same earnings season.
LME copper warehouse inventory rises above 350,000 tonnes on a 4-week rolling average (supply normalization signal).
invalidation_conditions.review_note: PENDING 4-WEEK CONFIRMATION (2026-06-04): latest single LME reading 393,400t (2026-05-20) is above the 350,000t threshold, but the condition specifies a 4-week rolling average which is not yet computed from a single data point. Not flipping 'triggered' to true on one print. However, given the magnitude (+12% above threshold) and positive inflows, the monthly review applies the review_and_reduce action pre-emptively: trim copper 6%→3.5% (see entry.position_size_rationale). Re-check the 4-week average at the next monthly cycle; if confirmed >350kt, set triggered=true.

invalidation_conditions.condition: EUR/USD spot falls below 1.02 and stays below for 10 consecutive trading days (FX loss amplification — position P&L in EUR severely impacted independently of copper thesis).

time_horizon.catalyst_maturation_note: The re-rating mechanism is narrative-driven: Goldman/JPM/BofA commodities desks updating DC copper demand from scenario to base case in their models. This historically happens 6-18 months after specialist reports establish the narrative (WoodMac published Q3 2025, Morgan Stanley Q4 2025). Expected window: Q3-Q4 2026 earnings cycle when hyperscalers report actual copper purchases and construction costs for the first time at scale. Maximum 52 weeks: if the narrative has not started shifting to generalist coverage by Q2 2027, the re-rating thesis is wrong on timing (though fundamental thesis may still hold).

tax.tax_notes: ⚠ UPDATE REQUIRED: ytd_realized_gains_eur_at_entry is set to 0 — replace with actual YTD realized gains before entry. If YTD gains are already above €6,000, the marginal bracket changes to 0.21. Current assumption: this is the first realized gain of the year. At 0% YTD, first €6k of this position's gain is taxed at 19%, anything above at 21%. At 6% portfolio allocation and a 50% gain, tax impact is manageable.
